source_title: VB-MAPP Verbal Behavior Milestones Assessment Protocol

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously mands for different verbal information using a WH question word 5 times
milestones.example: What's your name? Where do I go?

milestones.milestone_name: Politely mands to stop an undesirable activity, or remove any aversive MO under 5 different circumstances
milestones.example: Please stop pushing me. No thank you. Excuse me, can you move?

milestones.milestone_name: Mands with 10 different adjectives, prepositions, or adverbs
milestones.example: My crayon is broken. Don't take it out. Go fast.

milestones.milestone_name: Gives directions, instructions, or explanations as to how to do something or how to participate in an activity 5 times
milestones.example: You put the glue on first, then stick it. You sit here while I get a book.

milestones.milestone_name: Mands for others to attend to his own intraverbal behavior 5 times
milestones.example: Listen to me... I'll tell you... Here's what happened... I'm telling the story...

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts the color, shape, and function of 5 objects (15 trials) when each object and question is presented in a mixed order
milestones.example: What color is the refrigerator? What shape is the valentine? What do you do with the ball?
milestones.note: This is part tact and part intraverbal.

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts 4 different prepositions and 4 pronouns
milestones.example: Prepositions: in, out, on, under. Pronouns: I, you, me, mine.

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts 4 different adjectives, excluding colors and shapes, and 4 adverbs
milestones.example: Adjectives: big, little, long, short. Adverbs: fast, slow, quietly, gently.

milestones.milestone_name: Tacts with complete sentences containing 4 or more words, 20 times
Has a tact vocabulary of 1000 words (nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc.), tested or from an accumulated list of known tacts
Selects items by color and shape from an array of 6 similar stimuli, for 4 colors and 4 shapes
milestones.example: Find the red car. Find the square cracker.

milestones.milestone_name: Follows 2 instructions involving 6 different prepositions and 4 different pronouns
milestones.example: Stand behind the chair. Touch my ear.

milestones.milestone_name: Selects items from an array of similar stimuli based on 4 pairs of relative adjectives and demonstrates actions based on 4 pairs of relative adverbs
milestones.example: Relative adjectives: big-little, long-short. Relative adverbs: quiet-loud, fast-slow.

milestones.milestone_name: Follows 3-step directions for 10 different directions
milestones.example: Get your coat, hang it up, and sit down.

milestones.milestone_name: Has a total listener repertoire of 1200 words (nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc.), tested or from an accumulated list of known words
Spontaneously matches any part of an arts and crafts activity to another person's sample 2 times
milestones.example: A peer colors a balloon red and the child copies the peer's red color for his balloon.

milestones.milestone_name: Demonstrates generalized non-identical matching in a messy array of 10 with 3 similar stimuli, for 25 items
milestones.note: Matches new items on the first trial.

milestones.milestone_name: Completes 20 different block designs, parquetry, shape puzzles, or similar tasks with at least 8 different pieces
Sorts 5 items from 5 different categories without a model
milestones.example: Animals, clothing, furniture.

milestones.milestone_name: Continues 20 three-step patterns, sequences, or seriation tasks
milestones.example: Star, triangle, heart, star, triangle...

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously engages in pretend or imaginary play on 5 occasions
milestones.example: Dressing up, a pretend party with stuffed animals, pretends to cook.

milestones.milestone_name: Repeats a gross motor play behavior to obtain a better effect for 2 activities
milestones.example: Throwing a ball in a basket, swinging a bat at a T-ball, foot stomping to launch a rocket, pumping a swing.

milestones.milestone_name: Independently engages in arts and crafts type activities for 5 minutes
milestones.example: Drawing, coloring, painting, cutting, pasting.

milestones.milestone_name: Independently engages in sustained play activities for 10 minutes without adult prompts or reinforcement
milestones.example: Playing with an Etch-a-sketch, playing dress-up.

milestones.milestone_name: Independently draws or writes in pre-academic activity books for 5 minutes
milestones.example: Dot-to-dot, matching games, mazes, tracing letters and numbers.

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously cooperates with a peer to accomplish a specific outcome 5 times
milestones.example: One child holds a bucket while the other pours in water.

milestones.milestone_name: Spontaneously mands to peers with a WH question 5 times
milestones.example: Where are you going? What's that? Who are you being?

milestones.milestone_name: Intraverbally responds to 5 different questions or statements from peers
milestones.example: Verbally responds to What do you want to play?

milestones.milestone_name: Engages in pretend social play activities with peers for 5 minutes without adult prompts
milestones.example: Dress up play, acting out videos, playing house.

milestones.milestone_name: Engages in 4 verbal exchanges on 1 topic with peers for 5 topics
milestones.example: The children go back and forth talking about making a creek in a sandbox.

milestones.milestone_name: Attends to a book when a story is being read to him for 75% of the time
Selects (LDs) the correct uppercase letter from an array of 5 letters, for 10 different letters
Tacts 10 uppercase letters on command
Reads his own name
Matches 5 words to the corresponding pictures or items in an array of 5, and vice versa
milestones.example: Matches the written word bird to a picture of a bird.

milestones.milestone_name: Imitates 5 different writing actions modeled by an adult using a writing instrument and writing surface
Independently traces within 1/4 inch of the lines of 5 different geometrical shapes
milestones.example: Circle, square, triangle, rectangle, star.

milestones.milestone_name: Copies 10 letters or numbers legibly
Legibly spells and writes his own name without copying
Copies all 26 upper and lower case letters legibly

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects the correct item from an array of 10 that contains 3 similar stimuli, for 25 different WH question LRFFC tasks
milestones.note: The array contains 3 similar stimuli, such as the wrong color, shape, or class.

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects items from a book based on 2 verbal components: either a feature, function, or class, for 25 LRFFC tasks
milestones.example: Do you see a brown animal? Can you find some clothing with buttons?

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects items from a page in a book or in the natural environment based on 3 verbal components, for 25 WH question LRFFC tasks
milestones.example: Verb, adjective, preposition, pronoun. Which fruit grows on trees?

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Selects the correct items from a book or the natural environment given 4 different rotating LRFFC questions about a single topic for 25 different topics
milestones.example: Where does the cow live? What does the cow eat? Who milks the cow?

milestones.official_domain: Listener Responding by Function, Feature, and Class (LRFFC)
milestones.milestone_name: Demonstrates 1000 different LRFFC responses, tested or obtained from an accumulated list of known responses
Spontaneously emits 20 intraverbal comments (can be part mand)
milestones.example: Dad says, I'm going to the car, and the child spontaneously says, I want to go for a ride!

milestones.milestone_name: Demonstrates 300 different intraverbal responses, tested or obtained from an accumulated list of known intraverbals
Answers 2 questions after being read short passages (15+ words) from books, for 25 passages
milestones.example: Who blew the house down?

milestones.milestone_name: Describes 25 different events, videos, stories, etc. with 8+ words
milestones.example: Tell me what happened... The big monster scared everybody and they all ran into the house.

milestones.milestone_name: Answers 4 different rotating WH questions about a single topic for 10 topics
milestones.example: Who takes you to school? Where do you go to school? What do you take to school?

milestones.milestone_name: Uses the toilet and washes hands with only verbal prompts
Responds to 5 different group instructions or questions without direct prompts in a group of 3 or more children
milestones.example: Everybody stand up. Does anyone have a red shirt on?

milestones.milestone_name: Works independently for 5 minutes in a group, and stays on task for 50% of the period
Acquires 2 new behaviors during a 15-minute group-teaching format involving 5 or more children
Sits in a 20-minute group session involving 5 children without disruptive behaviors, and answers 5 intraverbal questions
Emits noun inflections by combining 10 root nouns with suffixes for plurals and 10 root nouns with suffixes for possessions
milestones.example: Dog vs. dogs. Dog's collar vs. cat's collar.

milestones.milestone_name: Emits verb inflections by combining 10 root verbs with affixes for regular past tense and 10 root verbs with affixes for future tense
milestones.example: Played. Will play.

milestones.milestone_name: Emits 10 different noun phrases containing at least 3 words, with 2 modifiers
milestones.example: Adjectives, prepositions, pronouns. He's my puppet. I want chocolate ice cream.

milestones.milestone_name: Emits 10 different verb phrases containing at least 3 words, with 2 modifiers
milestones.example: Adverbs, prepositions, pronouns. Push me hard. Go up the steps.

milestones.milestone_name: Combines noun and verb phrases to produce 10 different syntactically correct clauses or sentences containing at least 5 words
milestones.example: The dog licked my face.

milestones.milestone_name: Identifies as a listener the numbers 1-5 in an array of 5 different numbers
Tacts the numbers 1-5
Counts out 1-5 items from a larger set of items with 1 to 1 correspondence
milestones.example: Give me 4 cars. Now give me 2 cars.

milestones.milestone_name: Identifies as a listener 8 different comparisons involving measurement
milestones.example: Show me more or less, big or little, long or short, full or empty, loud or quiet.

milestones.milestone_name: Correctly matches a written number to a quantity and a quantity to a written number for the numbers 1-5
milestones.example: Matches the number 3 to a picture of 3 trucks.
